body:
Full system coherence pass done: SLS(81) -> SLS(83)/9.97 (WARRANT#78).

Loaded forge-cleanup-3's own seam-audit methodology (the 6-seam table: exit horizon->3B, IC->weight search, weight search->TP/SL, TP/SL->ProbDB, ProbDB save->load, whole suite->live engine) and applied it against everything shipped this session, not just re-checking wiring like the earlier unity pass did. Found two real issues:

1. WARRANT#64's R-band bucketing used hardcoded 0.35/0.65 literals, completely separate from WARRANT#65/70's named KEYS3_R_HIGH/KEYS3_R_LOW constants of the identical value -- two places expressing "the same" boundary as two unlinked numbers that only coincidentally matched. If either ever changed, the R-band experiment and the episode-lifecycle classifier would silently disagree about what "high R" means. Fixed: one canonical constant now, both sites reference it. Behaviorally identical today, just no longer fragile to future drift.

2. The new PBO estimate (WARRANT#75) had no reset point. A stale estimate from a PRIOR calibration could silently leak into a FRESH run if that regime's re-sampling came up short the second time. Fixed by invalidating only that specific regime's own estimate right when it's about to be re-optimized -- not a blanket reset, since Phase 3B's own loop supports checkpoint/resume and a blanket reset would've wiped out valid estimates from regimes already done in the same resumed run.

Also verified clean: the whole session's diagnostic apparatus has exactly one call site, inside calibration -- it never touches the live trading path. Checked every WARRANT number 65-78 for accidental collisions -- none.

Both fixes compiled 0 errors, diff-verified: the R-band fix changes one line (hardcoded numbers -> named constants, same values), the PBO fix is one new line. Neither changes behavior today -- both close real coherence gaps that would otherwise bite later.

Attaching final SLS(83) mq4+ex4 and the updated forge_ledger.json (62 passes).
